description: Mechanics built on cards and decks: constructing them, drafting them, holding them, playing them in patterns, collecting them, and manipulating their order.

deckbuilding.description: Growing and curating a personal deck or bag during play.

focuses.starter_deck_upgrades: Everyone begins with the same weak starter deck and buys better cards into it, gradually replacing filler with strategy (as in Dominion).
focuses.deck_thinning: Removing weak cards from your deck is as valuable as adding strong ones, concentrating the deck's quality (trashing via Chapel in Dominion).
focuses.market_row_purchase: New cards are bought from a shared face-up row that refills as cards leave it, making the buy menu unpredictable (as in Ascension or Star Realms).
focuses.fixed_supply_piles: Cards are purchased from fixed, known supply piles chosen at setup, so every buy option is visible all game (kingdom piles in Dominion).
focuses.deck_as_life: The deck doubles as a life total or unit roster, and losing or milling cards is taking damage (decking in Magic: The Gathering, casualties in Undaunted: Normandy).
focuses.bag_building: The deck-building loop uses tokens drawn from a bag instead of shuffled cards, with the same curation of a random container (as in Orléans or The Quacks of Quedlinburg).
focuses.deck_cycling_tempo: Players plan around when their deck will reshuffle, timing purchases and plays so key cards return at the right moment (hand cycling in Great Western Trail).
focuses.hybrid_deckbuilding: Deck-building fuels another system such as movement or worker placement, with card play driving board actions (as in Clank! or Dune: Imperium).
focuses.card_crafting_upgrades: Individual cards are physically and permanently upgraded during play, growing stronger while staying in the deck (card crafting in Mystic Vale).
focuses.no_shuffle_discard_order: Discards are never shuffled; the deck is turned back over in discard order, making play order a form of deck programming (as in Aeon's End).
focuses.asymmetric_starting_decks: Players begin with different starting decks or setups, giving each seat a distinct deck-building trajectory (mage decks in Aeon's End).

drafting.description: Distributing cards among players through structured picking.

focuses.pick_and_pass_draft: Players keep one card from a hand and pass the rest around the table until the packs are exhausted (as in 7 Wonders or Sushi Go!).
focuses.open_display_draft: Players pick from a shared face-up display with full information about what taking a card leaves behind (the bird tray in Wingspan).
focuses.snake_draft: Draft order reverses on each pass (1-2-3-3-2-1) so end-seat picks come in pairs, balancing positional advantage (as in fantasy sports drafts and many setup drafts).
focuses.pick_position_tradeoff: Choosing a better item costs position, pushing you later in the next selection order (tile selection in Kingdomino).
focuses.booster_pack_draft: Cards are drafted from sealed randomized packs, so evaluation happens against an unknown card pool (booster draft in Magic: The Gathering).
focuses.take_all_of_type_draft: Taking one item from a display forces you to take all items of that type there, making quantity and waste part of the pick (factory drafting in Azul).
focuses.sliding_cost_display: Cards slide along a display and become cheaper or richer the longer they remain, pricing popularity automatically (declining races in Small World, spice-laden cards in Century: Spice Road).
focuses.constrained_selection_draft: Only a limited window of upcoming items may be chosen, typically the next few in a line, so selection is a constrained lookahead (the three-patch rule in Patchwork).

hand.description: Holding, valuing, and spending the cards in your hand.

focuses.hand_management: Winning play comes from sequencing and husbanding a hand of cards, deciding what to play, hold, and discard when (as in Lost Cities).
focuses.hand_limits: A maximum hand size forces discards or blocks draws when exceeded (the seven-card robber limit in Catan).
focuses.multi_use_cards: Each card in hand can be played in several distinct capacities, and every use forgoes the others (as in Glory to Rome or Race for the Galaxy).
focuses.card_as_currency: Cards themselves are the money: costs are paid by discarding other cards from hand (as in San Juan or Race for the Galaxy).
focuses.discard_costs: Effects or actions require discarding cards as an activation cost distinct from normal play, so hand size is fuel as well as options (discarding a card for the space race in Twilight Struggle).
focuses.forced_play_order: Cards must be played in the order drawn or held, with no rearranging, so managing intake order is the game (bean planting in Bohnanza).
focuses.hand_replenishment: Hands refill by fixed draws, refill-to-a-limit, or by spending actions on drawing, making replenishment itself a tactical choice (drawing train cards in Ticket to Ride).
focuses.hidden_hand_information: Hands are private information, enabling bluffing, deduction, and surprise (as in poker or Tichu).
focuses.card_passing: Players pass cards directly to opponents or partners, transferring both value and information (passing in Hearts and Tichu).
focuses.inverted_hand_visibility: Players see everyone's cards except their own, inverting the normal flow of information (as in Hanabi).

play_patterns.description: Structured ways cards are played to the table: tricks, ladders, melds, and tableaus.

focuses.trick_taking: Players each contribute one card to a trick and the best card wins it, under constraints of suit and rank (as in Hearts or The Crew).
focuses.ladder_climbing_shedding: Players must beat the previous play with a higher card or combination or else pass, racing to shed their whole hand (as in Tichu or The Great Dalmuti).
focuses.melding_sets: Cards are laid down in valid groups or runs, converting a raw hand into scored structures (as in Rummy or Mahjong).
focuses.tableau_placement_cards: Cards are played into a personal spread whose contents persist and define your abilities and scoring (as in Race for the Galaxy or Terraforming Mars).
focuses.trump_suits: A designated suit outranks all others for the duration of a hand or round (as in Euchre or Wizard).
focuses.must_follow_suit: Players must play the led suit when able, turning hand composition into a constraint system (as in Bridge or The Crew).
focuses.trick_bidding: Players predict how many tricks they will win and score for accuracy rather than volume (as in Wizard or Skull King).
focuses.card_tucking: Cards are tucked under other cards, hiding their face value but contributing to counts or engines (tucking in Wingspan and Innovation).
focuses.card_splaying: The orientation or offset of stacked cards changes which icons are visible and active (splaying in Innovation).
focuses.spatial_card_layouts: Cards are placed into a spatial arrangement where adjacency and overlap determine scoring (as in Sprawlopolis or Honshu).

collection.description: Gathering cards into sets, contracts, and majorities for value.

focuses.set_collection: Players gather matching or complementary cards and score for completed groupings (as in Ticket to Ride).
focuses.contract_fulfillment: Specific demanded combinations must be assembled and turned in for rewards (destination tickets in Ticket to Ride, orders in Century: Golem Edition).
focuses.collection_majorities: Holding the most of a category at scoring time beats mere accumulation (building types in Alhambra).
focuses.nonlinear_set_scoring: Set value grows nonlinearly with size, so one large set is worth far more than several small ones (the 1-3-6-10 scoring in Coloretto).
focuses.negative_collection: Some cards are worth negative points, and play revolves around avoiding or dumping them (bull heads in 6 nimmt!, hearts in Hearts).
focuses.collection_discounts: Collected cards grant permanent discounts or powers during play, not just endgame points (gem bonuses in Splendor).
focuses.set_liquidation: Collected sets are sold during play at rates that reward larger or better-timed sales (selling goods in Jaipur, harvesting beans in Bohnanza).

deck_manipulation.description: Controlling the order, composition, and flow of decks during play.

focuses.scrying_reordering: Players peek at and reorder the top cards of a deck, converting uncertainty into planning (scry in Magic: The Gathering).
focuses.seeded_shuffles: Specific cards are deliberately shuffled into particular sections of the deck, guaranteeing paced arrival (epidemic seeding in Pandemic).
focuses.event_deck_escalation: The event deck intensifies over time, often by reshuffling the discard on top so recent threats recur (the infection deck in Pandemic).
focuses.deck_stacking_setup: Decks are assembled or ordered by design at setup for a scenario or difficulty level (encounter deck assembly in Arkham Horror: The Card Game).
focuses.deck_searching_tutors: Effects let players search the deck for a chosen card, trading tempo for certainty (tutors in Magic: The Gathering).
focuses.deck_flipping_push_luck: Cards are revealed one at a time from the deck and players choose when to stop, banking gains before a bust (as in Port Royal or Incan Gold).
focuses.deck_milling: Cards are removed from the top of a deck to a discard or removed pile as an attack or cost, draining that deck's future (milling in Magic: The Gathering).
focuses.multiple_deck_sources: Players draw from several distinct decks with different content profiles, choosing risk and reward by source (door and treasure decks in Munchkin).
focuses.discard_pile_retrieval: The discard pile is open for pickup or retrieval, making what you throw away both information and ammunition for others (drawing from the discard in Rummy and Canasta).
